title: docker incompatibility: commit --change is split on spaces.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

The `changes=` parameter of  `/commit` of the compat API is parsed incorrectly, making it impossible to use some dockerfile commands.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Send a request `POST /v1.40/commit?changes=EXPOSE+9999&container=$someid`

2. Alternatively use docker to speak to podman: 
`DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/podman.sock docker commit --change 'EXPOSE 9999' $someid`

**Describe the results you received:**

500 Internal Error
`CommitFailure: invalid change "EXPOSE" - must be formatted as KEY VALUE`

**Describe the results you expected:**

That it commits a new image with the changes applied.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This happens because the changes string is split on any whitespace, instead of newlines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2cdab5d53923784e72020d70ee9375518f19f9b6/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images.go#L140

Then `util.GetImageConfig` parses `EXPOSE` and `9999` separately and errors.

Note that the libpod API works correctly: 
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/images.go#L542

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.5
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body: Upon further investigation, it shows that libpod doesn't split the string at all, but expects multiple occurrences of the parameter. So it behaves differently than docker in yet another way.
Is this a different API IE the libpod api or the compat api or requiring multiple occurrences?

Official Docker API: one parameter, split on newline
Podman compat API: one parameter, split on whitespace (definitely wrong)
Podman libpod API: multiple parameter, not split at all

I guess the libpod API is ok, albeit different.
Care to open a PR to fix?
@leahneukirchen I believe the libpod API makes the most sense. But a fix for the compat API would be great. Are you interested or should I work on it?

There are more issues with the compat API. It seems to parse the body for a container config, but never uses it after:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2cdab5d53923784e72020d70ee9375518f19f9b6/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images.go#L131

I don't have time to go down this rabbit hole for now, I switched our software to use the libpod API for commit.
